description: Define a unified inbound queue job contract that supports provider-specific pointers for Microsoft, Google, and IMAP
Refactor Microsoft webhook ingress to enqueue pointer jobs instead of running full email processing inline
Refactor Google webhook ingress to enqueue pointer jobs instead of running full email processing inline
Refactor IMAP listener ingress to enqueue pointer jobs instead of submitting full email payloads
Use Redis as the durable temporary inbound queue backing store for unified pointer jobs
Ensure ingress endpoints/listeners return success only after durable enqueue succeeds
Return non-2xx ingress responses when enqueue fails so upstream systems retry
Implement unified queue consumer loop for claiming inbound jobs and executing processing
Implement consume-time provider fetch for full email payload based on pointer job data
Apply consume-time idempotency guard keyed by normalized external message identity before processing
Wire consumer to execute existing processInboundEmailInApp pipeline after payload fetch
Acknowledge queue jobs only after successful processing completion
Support resurfacing/reclaim of unacknowledged jobs for retry
Implement bounded retry policy with attempt tracking for failed jobs
Move jobs to dead-letter queue after max retry attempts
Handle source-unavailable pointer jobs with deterministic skipped outcome instead of infinite retry
Normalize provider-specific message identity fields into one idempotency key format
Ensure queue payload contract remains pointer-only and excludes raw MIME or attachment bytes
Retire or bypass legacy IMAP in-memory async queue path for production processing
Preserve existing webhook auth/verification behavior while moving to enqueue-only ingress
Add/align configuration flags for unified queue enablement and legacy-path disablement
Add structured logs for enqueue, consume, retry, ack, skip, and DLQ events
Update provider-specific callback contracts so business processing is no longer performed inline
Add queue consumer routing logic per provider type (Microsoft, Google, IMAP)
Document the unified queue architecture and local validation runbook in the plan scratchpad/docs
